title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London as a global expansion of its One More Hour initiative. The fund will help put on free or affordable social events for young people to address rising loneliness among Gen Z.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Program covers New York, Los Angeles and London.
Part of the global expansion of the One More Hour initiative.
Funds aim to enable free or affordable social events for young people.
Framed as a response to increasing loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: The initiative targets three major metro regions (NY, LA, London) — relevant geographies for regional infrastructure and service planning.
Corporate funding is being directed toward in-person social events for young people, indicating tech companies' role in local event ecosystems.
Organizers and local stakeholders in those cities may see increased activity from funded social groups as a result of this program.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your business. The talk emphasizes an end-to-end build with no graph expertise required.
bullets: Knowledge graphs address the gap where general AI agents don't know your company.
Shows automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Connects the graph to an AI agent so it understands organization-specific information.
No graph theory or query expertise required—aims for accessible deployment.
Provides an end-to-end demo and references a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: Enables AI agents that actually understand your organization's data—applicable when tailoring AI to grid/data-center needs.
Automatic graph construction reduces time and technical barriers for integrating internal documents and datasets.
No required graph expertise lowers training and staffing hurdles for adoption in operational environments.
An end-to-end demo offers a practical template decision-makers can evaluate for pilot deployments.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, notes that while energy incentives are often top of mind, they’re only part of the tax picture for sustainability. She highlights less obvious opportunities—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation—and urges a broader view to unlock meaningful tax value from sustainability investments.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the whole story for sustainability tax benefits.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives are another underused source of tax value.
R&D tax credits may apply to innovation efforts like reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader lens can reveal significant tax value.
takeaways: When evaluating site selection, include location-based incentives and state/local abatements in the financial analysis.
Assess sustainability projects for potential R&D tax credit eligibility, especially innovations in processes or systems.
Don’t limit tax planning to energy incentives—map the full range of local, state, and innovation-related credits.
Integrate tax-credit screening into sustainability investment decisions to capture additional value.

Message: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star.

Location for download: link in bio.

takeaways: Promotional CTA could drive short-term increases in user sign-ups—monitor authentication and onboarding capacity.

Ensure monitoring and alerting are tuned to detect traffic spikes originating from social promotions.
Confirm that content-delivery and link tracking systems can handle campaign-driven referral traffic.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… NVIDIA's GeForce Garage video highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community build contest, u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The description lists the full parts used, the GeForce host (Dwight), and links to the winning system specs and NVIDIA social channels.
bullets: Contest winner: u/JeffersonPutnam from r/buildapc.
Featured system: small-form-factor (Fractal Design Era 2) with a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition GPU.
Key components listed: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB DDR5-6000 RAM, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Host credited: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
Links provided to full PCPartPicker build and NVIDIA social channels.
takeaways: Compact builds are being paired with top-tier GPUs (RTX 5090) — ensure power delivery and cooling are evaluated when considering compact or edge deployments.
SFF power choices (Corsair SF1000) imply planning for high-wattage SFX form-factor PSUs if deploying similar systems at scale.
Use of a 240mm AIO in an SFF chassis highlights the need to assess liquid-cooling compatibility and serviceability for dense or compact node designs.

Platform choices (mini-ITX B850 motherboard + modern Ryzen CPU) show modern small-form-factor motherboards can support high-performance consumer GPUs—verify motherboard and CPU socket compatibility when standardizing hardware.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions on Google Cloud, which streamlined operations and boosted developer productivity. The engagement compressed TTEC's digital transformation timeline and helped unlock entirely new revenue streams for the business.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Developer productivity was improved through the deployed solutions.
The partnership compressed the company’s digital transformation timeline.
New revenue streams were unlocked as a result of the AI solutions.
takeaways: So what: Faster AI prototyping and deployment can shorten transformation timelines—consider cloud consulting to accelerate projects.
So what: Leveraging robust cloud infrastructure supports operational efficiency and higher developer productivity.
So what: Successful GenAI deployments can create new revenue opportunities—plan for monetization alongside technical buildout.
So what: Partnerships with cloud consulting can expand ambitions by enabling quicker, lower-risk experimentation.
